person.name: Idonea de Saye
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Idonea de Say was born circa 1328 at Sawbridge, England.

She married Sir John Clinton, 3rd Baron Clinton, Constable of Warwick Castle, son of Sir John de Clinton, 2nd Baron Clinton, Justice of the Peace for Warwickshire and Margaret Corbet, circa 1350. 

«b»They had 3 sons & 2 daughters:«/b»
1.) Sir William

2.) Sir Thomas

3.) Edward

4.) Margaret, wife of Sir Baldwin de Montfort

5.) Katherine, wife of Sir Thomas, 5th Lord Berkeley

6.) Idonea de Say died before 19 September 1379.

«b»Manor of Saye's Court«/b»
"Sir William Heron above mentioned possessed this manor in right of his wife, anno 19 Richard II. together with her, by the name of Elizabeth lady Saye, levied a fine of it, with all other their manors and lands in Kent, to the use of them and the heirs male of their bodies; remainder to her own right heirs. Four years after which she died, s. p. Upon which this manor came to Sir William Heron in her right, and he died possessed of it in the 6th year of king Henry IV. s. p. likewise, Sir John  son of his brother Sir John Heron, being his next heir, all which was found by inquisition taken here at Deptford, and that it was held in capite, and consisted of one capital messuage here and two hundred and twenty-five acres of land, and of rents of assize of free tenements seven pounds eight shillings and two leets, and it was likewise found by an inquisition, taken after her death, that she died, s. p. and that Sir William de Clinton, son of Idonea, sister of William de Saye last mentioned; Mary wife of Otho de Worthington, and daughter of Thomas de Aldon, by Elizabeth, another sister of the said William; and Maud, her sister; and Roger de Fiennes, [grand]son of William by Joane, another sister, who afterwards had Stephen de Valoines; were her heirs and next of kin. They most probably joined in the sale of it, for in the 3d year of Henry V. it was found, (fn. 14) that Sir John Philip and Alice his wife held the reversion of this manor, and that Sir William Philip was his brother and next heir."

«b»Inquisition of Elizabeth de Say«/b»
Writ 18 Nov. 1404.
BUCKINGHAM. Inquisition. Buckingham 28 Feb 1405
"She held nothing in Buckinghamshire. She died on 8 July 1399. Her next heirs are William de Clynton, knight, son of William de Clynton, knight, son of John de Clynton, knight, and Idonea his wife, one aunt of Elizabeth; Mary wife of Otto de Worthyngton, and Maud her sister, daughters of Thomas de Alden, knight, and Elizabeth his wife, another aunt; and Roger Fyenles, son of William de Fyenles, knight, son of William de Fyenles and Joan his wife, her third aunt. Idonea, Elizabeth and Joan were sisters of William de Say, knight, father of Elizabeth. William de Clynton is aged 26 years and more, Mary 34 years and more, and Maud 28 years and more. Roger will be 20 on 14 Sept. next."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Idonea de Saye於1328年出生在英國瓦維其可爾薩布利其時，她的父親Geoffrey De Saye II23歲，母親Maud de Beauchamp14歲。她於1350年與John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1384年6月26日之前在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Idonea de Saye于1328年出生在英国瓦維其可爾薩布利其时，她的父亲Geoffrey De Saye II23岁，母亲Maud de Beauchamp14岁。她于1350年与John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1384年6月26日之前在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: Idonea
meanings: An old baptismal name derived from Latin idoneus, fit, meet, proper.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Idonea de Saye was born in 1328, in Sawbridge, Warwickshire, England, her father, Geoffrey De Saye II, was 23 and her mother, Maud de Beauchamp, was 14. She married John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton in 1350, in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died before 26 June 1384, in England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton en 1350, à Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée avant 26 juin 1384, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Idonea de Sayeが1328年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアサウブリッジで生まれた時，父Geoffrey De Saye IIは23歳，母Maud de Beauchampは14歳であった。1350年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアマクスストクでJohn de Clinton 3rd Lord Clintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1384年6月26日以前にイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Idonea de Saye(이)가 1328년 영국 워윅실르 사w브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Geoffrey De Saye II(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Beauchamp(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 맥스스토크에서 John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1384년 6월 26일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Idonea de Saye родилась в 1328 г. в местности савбридж, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Geoffrey De Saye II было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Beauchamp, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Clinton 3rd Lord Clinton в 1350 г. в местности макссток, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла перед 26 июнь 1384 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания.
